title: Llama7B dense tile all-measured clustered schedule
hypothesis: Measured dense FP16 GEMM tile PPA should improve the Llama7B 131k clustered schedule frontier versus the older nm-count compute blocks, but the result must be checked with measured local attention, softmax, FIFO, and router overhead charged before using it as the current frontier.

direct_comparison.primary_question: When dense tile PPA from l1_npu_dense_gemm_tile_scaling_v2 is used inside the all-measured L1 clustered schedule model, which die/SRAM/logic/cluster point is selected and what abstraction remains?
include: dense_gemm_8x16_k1_p1, dense_gemm_16x8_k1_p1, and dense_gemm_16x16_k1_p2 metrics from the dense tile scaling result
cluster counts 1, 2, 4, 8, and 16 with owner-cluster and cluster-tree reduction
exclude: KV4, MQA, or other quality-unbacked KV reductions
follow_on_broad_sweep: replace analytic SRAM/global NoC service with a more detailed memory and communication simulator for the selected frontier candidates

expected_benefit: close the mismatch between the dense tile compute result and the clustered schedule frontier
show whether measured dense compute still reaches the practical HBM/memory frontier after measured L1 overhead is charged
produce a ranked candidate list for the next detailed SRAM/global NoC simulation job
risks: replica scaling still assumes dense tiles can be composed without extra global command-distribution overhead beyond the analytic command knobs
SRAM service and global NoC arbitration remain L2 model terms
vector throughput remains tied to MAC throughput by vector_ops_per_mac rather than separately measured for dense tile arrays

expected_result.reason: The result should identify the best dense tile compute source and clustered schedule point after charging measured L1 local datapath, softmax, FIFO, and router overhead.
